text: Chris Rock has highlighted the issue of 'driving while black', by posting a series of tweets documenting three traffic stops in seven weeks. He captioned a photo of the most recent stop with the words, "Stopped by the cops again wish me luck", along with a photo of him in his car. The tweets have sparked a debate about racial profiling by the police, with some users accusing Rock of race-baiting, and others praising him for documenting the stops. Blacks are 30% more likely to be pulled over by police than whites, according to figures reported by the Bureau of Justice Statistics in 2013.

Rock's tweets have garnered support from some, while others accused him of race-baiting. Actor Isaiah Washington suggested that Rock should "#Adapt" to avoid racial profiling, which sparked controversy. In the past, Rock has addressed this issue in sketches on his show and even brought a white friend along during a car ride with Jerry Seinfeld to avoid being targeted by the police. The conversations surrounding these incidents highlight the ongoing racial disparities in police treatment.
